text: Even in the thickest undergrowth you were whipped by the water as it rushed through. But the lightning stopped flashing, its fiery beams no longer flamed their way through the tops of the trees; the thunder moved away and there was only a distant rumbling to be heard before it was entirely silent. Now the rain became gentler.

The author said that studying Venus is a worthy pursuit despite the dangers it presents because it can help the Earth is something was top happen. Also, on the planet surface,temperatures average over 800 degrees Fahrenheit, and the atmospheric pressure is 90 times greater than what we experience in life as it says it in paragragh 3, line 3-6.This pargragh prove that the author supported his answer by saying that Earth and Venus are not the same. That the atmosperic pressure is 90 times greater than what we have experince on our planet which is Earth. 90 times greater is pretty dangerous for us human to go to Venus.Therefore, the conditions are far more extreme than any humans encounter on Earth; such as an environment would crush even a submarine accustomed to diving to the deepest parts of our ocean and would liquefy many metals as it says it on paragragh 3, line 6-9. This paragragh also prove that it is dangerous but still great to pursuit despite the dangers it presents. Also, notable, Venus has the hottest surface temperature of any planets in our solar systems, even though Mercury is closer to our sun. The other reason to why it was a great experience for them is that " The austronomers are fascinated by Venus because it may well once have been the most Earth-like planets on our solar system." As it says it on pararagragh 4, line 2-7. Futhermore, recall that Venus can sometimes be our nearest option for a planetary visit, a crutial consideration given the long time frames of space travel. The other dangerous part of the trip is that they are sending human to Venus and is allowing the scientists to float above the fray. Imagine a blimp-like vehicle hovering 30 or so miles above the roiling Venusian landscape. Just as " Our jet airplanes travel at a higher altitude to fly over many storms, a vihicles hovering over Venus would avoid the unfriendly ground conditions by staying up and out of their way." As it says it on paragragh 5, line 4-8. The reason to why the people is going to space is because they are making sure that everything is safe and that there won't be any problem in the future if something really happens and people wont get to safe the Earth on times if their werent any researchers or any types of scientists. This essay evaluted on how well the author supports his ideas, because striving to meet the challenge presented by Venus has value, not only because of the insight to be gained the planet itself, but also because human curiosity will likely lead us into many equally intimidating endeavors. Our travels on Earth and beyond should not be limited by dangers and doubt but should be expanded to meet the very edges of imagination and innovation.

Frank put the sack carefully to one side, then helped his brother up to the top of the tower. After that he tied the treasure sack to his belt, in order that he might have the full use of his two hands in descending the precarious ladder. They were so excited by their momentous discovery, by the knowledge that all the days of fruitless search had now ended, that they descended the ladder at break-neck speed.

Luke had many reasons to join the program. He wanted to help countries recover their food supplies, animals, and more. The text read ,"It was 1945, World War 2 was over in Europe, and many countries were left in the ruins. To help these countries recover their food supplies, animals, and more, 44 nations joined together to form UNRRA ( the United Nations Relief and Rehabilitation Administration). It took some ti e to cross from place to place to help out these countries but it was no problem for the team. The passege read," It took about two weeks to cross the Alantic Ocean from the easter coast of the United States and a month to get to china." Even tho it took time to cross Luke had things to keep him busy. The author wrote," Caring for the animals during the crossing kept Luke busy. They had to be fed and watered two or three times a day. Bales of hay and bags of oats had to be pulled up from the lower holds of the ship. Stalls had to be cleaned." Luke incountered many different chalenges fduring the crossings. The text read," Helping out on his aunt Katie's farm as a boy had prepared Luke for hard work, but not for the dangers at sea." Luke was a hard worker on the boats and he did his duties. On his second trip, he got hurt and had to take it easy for a couple days. The text read," One rainy night after making his hourly report to animals every hour he slid down a slippery ladder on his backside. A small strip of metal along the edge stopped his slide keeping him from flying overboard into the dark Atlantic. He was happy to be alive but he couldn't work for a couple of days because of cracked ribs." Even after he got hurt he still stayed with the team. I think he was a very hard working team mate and he cared about other living things.
